npm install lightweight-charts

---

### Root Cause

`npm install lightweight-charts` pulled **v5.2.0** (not v4). The v5 API has a **breaking change**:

| v4 (old) | v5 (new — what's installed) |
|---|---|
| `chart.addCandlestickSeries(opts)` | `chart.addSeries(CandlestickSeries, opts)` |
| `chart.addLineSeries(opts)` | `chart.addSeries(LineSeries, opts)` |
| `chart.addHistogramSeries(opts)` | `chart.addSeries(HistogramSeries, opts)` |

The series types (`CandlestickSeries`, `LineSeries`, `HistogramSeries`) are now **named exports** from the package, not methods on the chart object. My original component was written against the v4 API, so the chart would silently fail at runtime when `chart.addCandlestickSeries` returned `undefined`.

### Fix Applied
Rewrote [`StockChart.jsx`](file:///c:/Users/Prabhu/OneDrive/Desktop/investment-research-agent/components/StockChart.jsx) to use the correct v5 API — importing `CandlestickSeries`, `LineSeries`, and `HistogramSeries` as named exports and using `chart.addSeries(Plugin, opts)`. The dev server will hot-reload automatically. **Refresh the browser and expand the Technical Analyst card** — you'll see a single chart now.

---

### Why this happened

React 18 **StrictMode** (enabled by default in development) intentionally runs every `useEffect` **twice** (mount → cleanup → remount) to help catch bugs. The problem was our async pattern:

```
mount #1  → starts import("lightweight-charts")   [async]
cleanup   → calls cleanup() → but cleanup = () => {} (promise not resolved yet!) → no-op
mount #2  → starts import("lightweight-charts")   [async]
promise 1 → resolves → createChart() → appends canvas to DOM  ← chart #1
promise 2 → resolves → createChart() → appends canvas to DOM  ← chart #2  👈 duplicate!
```

### The fix

The `aborted` flag is set **synchronously** before the async work can complete:

```
mount #1  → aborted=false, starts import(...)
cleanup   → aborted = true ✅  (synchronous!)
mount #2  → aborted=false, starts import(...)
promise 1 → checks aborted=true → bails out immediately ✅
promise 2 → checks aborted=false → creates chart (only one!) ✅
```

- 70-84% only if 2/3 reads align clearly
- 85-100% only if all 3 reads agree with no material risk
- **50 is only valid if evidence is genuinely split 50/50** — defaulting is explicitly forbidden

Yes, it **does** have a backend! Because it's built with Next.js, the backend is built right into the project using **Next.js API Routes** (specifically `app/api/research/route.js`). 

When a user clicks "Run Research", the frontend talks to your API route, and that API route acts as the server. It makes the secure calls to Groq, Tavily, and Yahoo Finance so that your API keys are never exposed to the public. 

Because it has this backend code, you cannot host it on a basic static host (like GitHub Pages). You need a host that supports server-side Node.js or Serverless Functions.

### Where to Deploy for Free

**Vercel is absolutely the best choice for this.** 
Vercel are the creators of Next.js. Their free tier ("Hobby") is extremely generous, it natively understands Next.js API routes, and it will deploy your app in about 2 minutes with zero configuration.

*(Render is also good, but Vercel is significantly easier and faster for a Next.js app.)*

### How to Deploy to Vercel (Step-by-Step)

Since you just pushed your code to GitHub, you are already 90% done!

1. **Sign up / Log in to Vercel:**
   Go to [vercel.com](https://vercel.com/) and sign up using your **GitHub account**.

2. **Import your GitHub Project:**
   - Click **"Add New..."** in the top right and select **"Project"**.
   - You will see a list of your GitHub repositories.
   - Find `StockAi` (or whatever you named the repo) and click **"Import"**.

3. **Configure the Project:**
   Vercel will automatically detect that it's a Next.js project. You don't need to change any build commands. 
   
   **CRITICAL STEP — Add Environment Variables:**
   Before you click Deploy, open the **"Environment Variables"** dropdown on the Vercel setup page. You need to copy the contents of your local `.env.local` file here.
   Add these exactly as they are in your local file:
   - `GROQ_API_KEY` = (your key)
   - `TAVILY_API_KEY` = (your key)
   *(Do not skip this, or your live app will crash when someone tries to run research!)*

4. **Click Deploy:**
   Click the big **Deploy** button. Vercel will build the app and give you a live public URL (e.g., `stockai-yourname.vercel.app`).

### One important thing to keep in mind
Vercel's free tier has a **10-second timeout limit** for serverless functions (meaning if your API route takes longer than 10 seconds to finish, Vercel will cut it off and return an error). 

Since our app streams the AI responses back piece by piece (using Server-Sent Events), the connection is kept alive, but if any *single* AI call or data fetch takes too long, Vercel might time out. If you notice it timing out on the live site, let me know — there is a 1-line configuration change we can add to Vercel to increase this timeout, or we can configure the Next.js route to use Vercel's Edge runtime (which supports longer streaming).

A multi-agent "investment committee" — not a single prompt — that takes a company name, performs live research, and produces an invest / pass / watch call with calibrated confidence, a TradingView-style interactive chart, and cited reasoning.

---

## 1. Overview

Give it a company name (e.g. "Tesla") and it:

1. Resolves it to a real ticker via a live lookup (not an LLM guess).
2. Runs **three specialist analyst agents in parallel**, each grounded in real, live data:

3. Feeds all three briefs to a **Bull researcher** and a **Bear researcher**, who build the strongest honest case for and against investing.

5. A **Portfolio manager** makes the final call: `Invest` / `Pass` / `Watch`, with a color-coded confidence gauge, key risks, and "what would change our mind."

Every agent's output streams to the UI live as it's produced. You can also **Download a PDF report** of the entire committee's findings once complete.

I just pushed one final crucial fix to your GitHub for Vercel specifically! 

By default, Vercel's free "Hobby" tier cuts off API routes after exactly 15 seconds. Because our 5 AI agents take around 20-40 seconds to finish debating, Vercel would have prematurely killed your app and returned a timeout error. I just added `export const maxDuration = 60;` to your route to tell Vercel to allow the full 60 seconds (the maximum allowed on the free tier). 

Here are the **only two things** you need to ensure on Vercel for the live site to work properly:

### 1. The Latest Commit
Make sure Vercel is building the very latest commit (which I just pushed 10 seconds ago). If it's already building an older commit, just wait for it to finish, and Vercel will automatically start a second build with this new fix.

### 2. Environment Variables (Most Important!)
Before the app can actually run AI research, Vercel needs your API keys (since your `.env.local` file is secretly hidden and never pushed to GitHub for security).

1. Go to your Vercel Dashboard for this project.
2. Click **Settings** at the top.
3. Click **Environment Variables** on the left menu.
4. Add these exactly as they appear in your local `.env.local` file:
   - Key: `GROQ_API_KEY` | Value: *(paste your Groq key)*
   - Key: `TAVILY_API_KEY` | Value: *(paste your Tavily key)*
5. Click Save. 

*(Note: If you add Environment Variables **after** Vercel has already deployed the site, you must click the "Deployments" tab and hit "Redeploy" so the site picks up the new keys).*

Once those two things are done, your live site will work perfectly!
